name: Hickory-Smoked Chicken
recipeIngredient: 2 teaspoons Chopped fresh rosemary leaves or crushed dried rosemary
1 tablespoon Chopped fresh rosemary leaves or crushed dried rosemary
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak wood chips in water for about 30 minutes.
2. Rub the cavity of the chicken with poultry seasoning and 2 teaspoons of rosemary.
3. Fill the cavity with orange slices, celery, and onions.
4. Fasten the opening with skewers if necessary.
5. Tie or skewer the drumsticks to the tail.
6. Fold the wings across the back with tips touching.
7. Insert a meat thermometer so the tip is in the thickest part of the inside thigh muscle or the thickest part of the breast meat, without touching the bone.
8. Arrange hot coals around the edge of the grill.
9. Place a foil drip pan under the grilling area.
10. Drain half of the wood chips and add them to the hot coals.
11. Stir 1 tablespoon of rosemary into melted margarine.
12. Place the chicken, breast side up, on the grill over the drip pan about 4 inches from medium coals.
13. Cover and grill for 3 hours or 3 hours 30 minutes, brushing frequently with the margarine mixture, until the thermometer registers 180°F to 185°F.
14. If necessary, add remaining drained wood chips and coals every 30 minutes to maintain smoke and even heat.
